solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int findBestValue(int[] arr, int target) {
        Arrays.sort(arr);
        int n = arr.length;
        int left = 0, right = arr[n - 1];
        int bestValue = right;
        int minDiff = Math.abs((arr[0] * n) - target);

        for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
            int sum = (arr[i] * (n - i)) + sumArray(arr, 0, i - 1);
            int diff = Math.abs(sum - target);
            if (diff < minDiff) {
                minDiff = diff;
                bestValue = arr[i];
            } else if (diff == minDiff) {
                bestValue = Math.min(bestValue, arr[i]);
            }
        }

        return bestValue;
    }

    private int sumArray(int[] arr, int start, int end) {
        int sum = 0;
        for (int i = start; i <= end; i++) {
            sum += arr[i];
        }
        return sum;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution sorts the array and iterates through it to find the best value by comparing the absolute difference between the target and the modified sum. Sorting ensures we can efficiently calculate the sum of elements less than or equal to the current value. The key strength is simplicity, but the sorting step increases time complexity.>
